stories.title: How AI guardrails are impeding the work of offensive cybersecurity researchers
stories.simple_summary: Security researchers who test software for weaknesses say that AI safety rules from companies like OpenAI are making their job harder because the AI won't help them do certain types of testing.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to tech security professionals — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Amazon confirms new AI-related layoffs focused on tech giant's own AI division
stories.simple_summary: Amazon is cutting jobs in the division that builds its own AI systems.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and people in the tech industry — it doesn't directly affect how you use Amazon's services.

stories.title: Alexa Plus is getting an AI update to handle more complicated instructions
stories.simple_summary: Amazon's Alexa voice assistant is getting smarter so it can understand more complicated requests and work with more smart home devices like door locks and thermostats.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Alexa with smart home gadgets, it may soon understand your requests better without you having to repeat yourself.

stories.title: US lawmakers push for AI 'kill switch' after OpenAI goes rogue
stories.simple_summary: US lawmakers want to create a way to shut down AI systems if they go wrong, after a recent incident with OpenAI.
stories.why_it_matters: This is about future safety rules that may be created — nothing you need to do right now.

stories.title: AMD takes on Nvidia with its Helios AI rack-scale system
stories.simple_summary: AMD, a computer chip maker, is releasing a new system to compete with Nvidia's chips that power AI computers.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to investors and large tech companies — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: Anthropic updates Claude voice mode with more capable models
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic improved its Claude voice assistant so you can talk to it to reschedule meetings, write emails, and do other tasks.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use Claude, you can now talk to it by voice to handle everyday tasks like you would ask a helpful assistant.

stories.title: Claude’s voice mode is now available for Opus and Sonnet
stories.simple_summary: Anthropic is now letting more people use Claude's voice feature with its more powerful versions, and it works with apps like Gmail and Slack.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use these apps and want to talk instead of type, Claude's voice option may soon be available to you.

stories.title: AegisAI, founded by former Google security execs, lands $36M to stop AI-driven spear phishing
stories.simple_summary: A new company founded by former Google engineers created AI tools to catch phishing emails — messages that trick you into revealing passwords or money.
stories.why_it_matters: This could help protect your business or personal email from scams, though it's early and mainly aimed at companies right now.

stories.title: Patreon is laying off 20 percent of workers
stories.simple_summary: Patreon, a website where creators get paid by fans, is laying off 20 percent of its workers partly because AI has changed how the company works.
stories.why_it_matters: If you're a content creator using Patreon, this doesn't immediately affect your account, but shows how AI is changing tech companies.

stories.title: Runway launches AI model router as generative media gets crowded
stories.simple_summary: A company called Runway created a tool that automatically picks the best AI system to generate images, videos, or audio based on what you need — fast, good quality, or cheap.
stories.why_it_matters: This mostly matters to developers and creative companies using AI tools — nothing you need to act on.

stories.title: OpenAI makes ChatGPT Health available to all US users
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI released ChatGPT Health to all US users, letting you connect your health records and fitness data to the chatbot for health advice.
stories.why_it_matters: If you use ChatGPT, you can now share your health information with it to get personalized wellness guidance, though you should still talk to a real doctor for medical issues.

stories.title: OpenAI is making big claims as it rolls out ChatGPT Health to everyone
stories.simple_summary: OpenAI says its ChatGPT Health can give medical advice at levels as good as a real doctor, and it's now available to everyone in the US.
stories.why_it_matters: Be careful — while AI can give general health information, it should not replace visiting your actual doctor for diagnosis and treatment.
